summary: Russian forces reported liberating Dobropasovo in Dnepropetrovsk region. Ukraine’s ground forces command building and the Lukyanovka plant were struck by Russian attacks. Multiple UAV and missile attacks continue across several fronts, with heightened threat warnings for Kyiv.
key_points: Russian troops have liberated the village of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian forces struck the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka plant, previously damaged (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Ukrainian forces in the village of Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia region, have held the line for over 1500 days, recognized by a national record (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian missile strikes hit Derhachi, causing 19 casualties and one death, and damaged a civilian enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Russian forces dropped five FAB-250 bombs on Kramatorsk, injuring at least two and damaging multiple buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a high threat of a mass missile attack on Kyiv and Kyiv region within three days, listing possible missile types (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced then later withdrawn (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces have taken Dobropasovo in Dnepropetrovsk, while Ukrainian command facilities and industrial sites have been struck. UAV and missile attacks persist across multiple fronts, with a warned mass strike risk on Kyiv. Ukrainian defenders continue to hold key positions such as Mala Tokmachka.

points: Ukrainian Ground Forces command building was hit by Russian forces, though the facility had not been used since the war began, according to Defense Minister advisor Sergey Beskrestnov (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
The Lukyanovka plant, previously damaged, was also struck, likely referring to the Artem missile‑production plant (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Ukrainian troops defending the village of Mala Tokmachka in Zaporizhzhia have maintained the line for over 1500 days, earning a national record (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
In Derhachi, a Russian rocket attack injured 19 civilians, killed a 68‑year‑old man, and damaged a civilian enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
In Kramatorsk, Russian forces dropped five FAB‑250 bombs, causing injuries and damage to residential and administrative buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 246 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Iltamass types, across the country on 25 May (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a high probability of a mass missile strike on Kyiv within three days, listing specific missile systems that could be used (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian forces conducted guided aviation bomb releases over Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/62732).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced (Source: @kpszsu/62758).
The same threat was later declared cancelled (Source: @kpszsu/62735).
Two Tu‑95MS aircraft were relocated from Olenya airfield to the Far East (Source: @eRadarrua/93655).
Russian officials cited a planned provocation involving the Czech‑detained Metropolitan Ilarion (Source: @tass_agency/377500).
Russia claimed a downed gas tanker “Arktik Metagaz” was damaged near Malta, accusing Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81452).
UAV activity reported over Kharkiv, Sumy, and Poltava directions, including groups heading toward Kovyagi, Sharivka, and Romny (Source: @kpszsu/62755, @kpszsu/62746).
Russian guided aviation bomb releases targeted Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/62732).
Ukrainian forces reported high‑speed reactive UAVs (>500 km/h) over Kyiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving from Kharkiv toward Donetsk, Balakliia, and other points (Source: @kpszsu/62763).
UAVs were sighted moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa direction (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
UAVs were reported heading from Sumy toward Romny at speeds over 600 km/h (Source: @eRadarrua/93628).
UAVs were observed over Zaporizhzhia moving toward Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62761).
UAVs detected over Chernihiv region near Horodnya from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93676).
UAVs observed over Sumy heading toward Lipova Dolyna en route to Poltava (Source: @kpszsu/62750).
UAVs reported from Kharkiv heading north toward Poltava, passing Kotelva (Source: @kpszsu/62711).
UAV activity noted over Kyiv region with high‑speed reactive drones (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).
UAVs were seen moving from Kharkiv toward the north‑west direction (Source: @kpszsu/62755).
UAVs reported over Sumy heading toward Nizy (Source: @kpszsu/62763).
UAVs observed from Kharkiv heading toward the city itself (Source: @kpszsu/62755).
Russian missile strikes hit Derhachi in Kharkiv region, causing civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Five FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk, injuring civilians and damaging buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
UAVs were reported moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa direction (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
UAVs observed over Zaporizhzhia moving toward Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62761).
UAVs reported in the southern direction over Sumy toward Romny (Source: @kpszsu/62746).
UAVs were sighted heading from Kharkiv toward Balakliia and other southern points (Source: @eRadarrua/93681).
A threat of ballistic weapons from the south was announced then withdrawn (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 246 hostile UAVs of various types on 25 May (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
Russian guided aviation bomb releases were conducted over Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/62732).
Two Tu‑95MS strategic bombers relocated from Olenya to the Far East (Source: @eRadarrua/93655).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of possible use of X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr, Kinzhal, and limited UAV attacks on Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Ukrainian Patriot‑equivalent systems are claimed to be able to intercept Russian ballistic missiles by year‑end (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30419).
Russian forces used FAB‑250 bombs on Kramatorsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian forces reported high‑speed reactive UAVs (>500 km/h) over Kyiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/93689).

key_developments: Russian troops have liberated the village of Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnepropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian forces struck the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka plant, which had previously been damaged (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
Ukrainian defenders in Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia region, have maintained the front for over 1500 days, earning a national record (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian missile strikes on Derhachi caused 19 civilian injuries and one death, damaging a civilian enterprise (Source: @UkraineNow/81448).
Five FAB‑250 bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk, injuring civilians and damaging residential and administrative structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81444).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a high threat of a mass missile attack on Kyiv within three days, listing specific missile systems that could be used (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced and subsequently withdrawn (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are advancing on the eastern front, exemplified by the liberation of Dobropasovo, while intensifying strikes on Ukrainian command and industrial targets. Ukraine faces a multi‑layered aerial threat, with high‑speed UAVs and potential mass missile attacks on Kyiv, though the ballistic threat has been temporarily withdrawn. Continued UAV activity across northern and southern fronts complicates air defense allocations and strains Ukrainian response capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing defenses on critical fronts, highlighted by the record‑holding defense of Mala Tokmachka and extensive UAV interceptions. International partners, including the European Commission, have pledged additional air‑defense assistance, while Russian diplomatic actions focus on propaganda and provocation narratives. Civilian casualties from missile strikes in Derhachi and Kramatorsk underscore the need for heightened protection of populated areas.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian intelligence has identified specific missile types that could be employed in a forthcoming Kyiv strike, enabling pre‑emptive alerting of air defense units. UAV movement patterns indicate coordinated Russian reconnaissance and strike missions across multiple axes, requiring adaptive counter‑UAV measures. The temporary suspension of ballistic weapon threats suggests possible Russian operational recalibration or diplomatic signaling.
